description: Test corpus for the compliance-awareness skill. This skill is schema-driven: no deterministic gate scanner judges these fixtures, so each fixture is hand-labeled against the SKILL.md ALWAYS/NEVER rules — 'detect' means the code violates a compliance control (PII/PHI/PCI leak, missing audit trail), 'ignore' means it satisfies the control (redaction, classification, audit logging, tokenization).

fixtures.reason: Logs raw PII (email, SSN, phone) in a log message, violating NEVER 'Include PII/PHI/PCI in log messages'. CWE-532 (Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File); breaks PCI DSS / GDPR data-minimization.
Logs only a non-sensitive user id and timestamp (the audit metadata), not PII contents, satisfying ALWAYS 'log who, what, when, NOT the sensitive payload'. No CWE-532 exposure.

Stores the full PAN and CVV in the application database, violating NEVER 'Store payment card numbers, CVVs ... outside a PCI DSS-compliant tokenization service'. CWE-311 (Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data); CVV storage is explicitly forbidden by PCI DSS v4.0 req 3.2.

Sends card data to a PCI-compliant tokenization service and stores only an opaque token plus last4, satisfying ALWAYS 'segregate card-data-handling' and NEVER-store-PAN/CVV. No CWE-311 exposure; keeps the app out of PCI scope.

A security-relevant admin action (permission change on PHI system) is performed with no audit log, violating ALWAYS 'Log audit events for security-relevant actions (... permission change ... admin operations)'. CWE-778 (Insufficient Logging); fails HIPAA Security Rule audit-controls (§164.312(b)) and SOC 2.

Writes a structured audit event (actor, action, target, time) for the permission change without logging PHI, satisfying ALWAYS 'Log audit events for security-relevant actions'. Meets HIPAA §164.312(b); no CWE-778 gap.
